# submitit root oneshot

Wrap `submitit.LocalExecutor`, create a session, and update its parameters.

## Why This Case Exists

Engineering workflows often end at job submission or orchestration, so the challenge suite needs at least one scheduler-oriented object case.

## Case Shape

- ID: `submitit_root`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `package`
- Workflow Slice: `hpc`
- Target Kind: `package`
- Upstream Target: `submitit`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/submitit_root/challenge.toml`

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=submitit_root
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only submitit_root
```

## Fixtures

This case does not need checked-in fixtures.

## Smoke Flow

### 1. Create a LocalExecutor session

Tool: `create_local_executor`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "folder": "{challenge_artifact_dir}/submitit"
}
```

Captures: `{'local_executor_session_id': 'session_id'}`

### 2. Update executor parameters

Tool: `local_executor_update_parameters`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "args": [],
  "kwargs": {
    "timeout_min": 1
  },
  "session_id": "{local_executor_session_id}"
}
```

### 3. Close the LocalExecutor session

Tool: `close_local_executor`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "session_id": "{local_executor_session_id}"
}
```

## What This Case Proves

- One-shot package ingestion can wrap an HPC-adjacent executor class.
- The generated session facade can call parameter-update methods with keyword arguments.
- The deterministic wrapper path can reach beyond solvers into orchestration tooling.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: wrap an HPC-oriented executor object from a scheduler helper library.
# This keeps the challenge close to real orchestration work without requiring a cluster.
id = "submitit_root"
title = "submitit root oneshot"
tier = "local_full"
style = "package"
slice = "hpc"

[example]
summary = "Wrap `submitit.LocalExecutor`, create a session, and update its parameters."
motivation = "Engineering workflows often end at job submission or orchestration, so the challenge suite needs at least one scheduler-oriented object case."
proves = [
  "One-shot package ingestion can wrap an HPC-adjacent executor class.",
  "The generated session facade can call parameter-update methods with keyword arguments.",
  "The deterministic wrapper path can reach beyond solvers into orchestration tooling.",
]

[target]
kind = "package"
value = "submitit"

[probe]
imports = ["submitit"]

[scaffold]
kind = "package"
symbol_include_patterns = ["^LocalExecutor$"]
max_generated_tools = 10

[smoke]
[[smoke.steps]]
label = "Create a LocalExecutor session"
tool = "create_local_executor"
arguments = { folder = "{challenge_artifact_dir}/submitit" }
capture_json = { local_executor_session_id = "session_id" }

[[smoke.steps]]
label = "Update executor parameters"
tool = "local_executor_update_parameters"
arguments = { session_id = "{local_executor_session_id}", args = [], kwargs = { timeout_min = 1 } }

[[smoke.steps]]
label = "Close the LocalExecutor session"
tool = "close_local_executor"
arguments = { session_id = "{local_executor_session_id}" }
```
